message.content:
You are a bug-bounty lead reporting to the Chia Network program (HackerOne). Findings below survived an adversarial panel. Produce a prioritized, payout-aware report.

INSTRUCTIONS: rank by severity x reachability x payout. Assign CVSS:3.1 vectors. Use web search to confirm the Chia Network HackerOne program is active and its rough payout tiers; put conclusions in program_scope_notes (say if a tool is unavailable). Be conservative and defensible; if empty, record the null result and name the deepest areas to revisit. For each finding give why_high_value, a concrete suggested_next_step (PoC/repro), and program_payout_note.

FINDINGS JSON:
[
  {
    "title": "SSRF: DataLayer node fetches attacker-controlled mirror URLs taken from unauthenticated on-chain coin spends",
    "file": "chia/data_layer/download_data.py",
    "line": 313,
    "vuln_class": "SSRF",
    "cwe": "CWE-918",
    "severity": "Medium",
    "source": "On-chain CREATE_COIN memos of a mirror coin (P2_PARENT.curry(1) puzzle, no owner/signature check). Parsed in chia/wallet/db_wallet/db_wallet_puzzles.py:get_mirror_info() lines 107-109 (memos[1:] -> url list) and stored unvalidated by chia/data_layer/data_layer_wallet.py:coin_added() lines 780-795 via Mirror.decode_urls().",
    "sink": "aiohttp ClientSession.get(server_info.url + \"/\" + filename, proxy=proxy_url) in http_download(), chia/data_layer/download_data.py lines 313-318.",
    "data_flow": "Any party spends a coin on-chain creating a mirror coin (create_mirror_puzzle, anyone-can-create; amount can be minimal) whose CREATE_COIN memos encode launcher_id + arbitrary URL strings. When a victim DL node tracks that launcher_id (it tracks every store it subscribes to via DataLayer.subscribe -> dl_track_new, or that it owns), DataLayerWallet.coin_added() (data_layer_wallet.py:774-796) sees the mirror coin, calls get_mirror_info() to extract the URLs with zero validation, and stores them via dl_store.add_mirror(). The periodic DataLayer.periodically_manage_data() loop runs DataLayer.update_subscription() (data_layer.py:1076-1089) for every subscription, which calls update_subscriptions_from_wallet() (data_layer.py:953-959): it pulls all mirrors via wallet_rpc.dl_get_mirrors and writes their URLs straight into the subscriptions table (data_store.update_subscriptions_from_wallet, data_store.py:1645). It then calls fetch_and_validate() (data_layer.py:607-687) which reads those URLs via get_available_servers_for_store() and, for each, calls insert_from_delta_file() -> download_file() (download_data.py:110-145, with downloader None for plain HTTP) -> http_download() (download_data.py:298-318) issuing aiohttp GET to server_info.url + '/' + filename. No scheme allowlist (http/https only enforced implicitly by aiohttp), no private/link-local IP filtering, no DNS-rebinding protection, and aiohttp follows redirects by default. filename is constrained (bytes32 hex + fixed suffix) but the host/scheme/path-prefix of the URL are fully attacker-controlled.",
    "exploitability": "Remote, unauthenticated on-chain actor. Largely blind SSRF over HTTP(S): the node will issue GET requests to internal addresses (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/... cloud metadata, internal admin HTTP services, localhost-only ports), follow redirects, and the downloaded body is written to a size-capped file (max_delta_file_size, default 250 MiB) rather than returned to the attacker. Impact is internal-service request forgery / internal port reachability / metadata-endpoint probing and forced outbound connections from the node, plus a controllable target for failed-download bookkeeping. Not arbitrary file read (aiohttp does not honor file://).",
    "votes": "1/1",
    "verifier_notes": [
      {
        "real": true,
        "refuted": false,
        "reachable": true,
        "mitig": false,
        "sev": "Medium",
        "sketch": "Blind SSRF via unauthenticated on-chain mirror coins. The DataLayer mirror puzzle create_mirror_puzzle() = P2_PARENT.curry(1) (chia/wallet/db_wallet/db_wallet_puzzles.py:90-94) is anyone-can-create with no owner key or signature, by design (mirrors are permissionless host announcements).\n\nConcrete exploit:\n1. Victim runs a chia data_layer service and has subscribed to some store launcher_id L (the normal DL use case). subscribe() -> wallet_rpc.dl_track_new() inserts L into singleton_records, so is_launcher_tracked(L) returns True (dl_wallet_store.py:293-302).\n2. Attacker spends ANY coin they control on-chain, producing a CREATE_COIN to MIRROR_PUZZLE_HASH with a small amount (e.g. 1 mojo). The spend's CREATE_COIN memo list is [L, b\"http://169.254.169.254:80\", ...] -- launcher_id followed by arbitrary URL byte strings. No signature tying the spend to L's owner is required.\n3. The victim wallet observes the new mirror coin. DataLayerWallet.coin_added() (data_layer_wallet.py:773-796) fetches the parent spend, runs get_mirror_info() (db_wallet_puzzles.py:97-110) which returns (L, memos[1:]) with zero validation, checks only is_launcher_tracked(L)==True, and stores Mirror.decode_urls(urls) (plain utf-8 decode, no checks) via dl_store.add_mirror().\n4. On the next periodically_manage_data() cycle (default 60s), update_subscription() -> update_subscriptions_from_wallet() (data_layer.py:953-959) pulls the attacker URLs via dl_get_mirrors and writes them straight into the subscriptions table.\n5. fetch_and_validate() (data_layer.py:607+) reads them through get_available_servers_for_store() and calls insert_from_delta_file() -> download_file() (downloader=None for plain http, so http_download) -> http_download() (download_data.py:298-318) issues aiohttp session.get(server_info.url + \"/\" + filename). filename is the fixed-format \"{store_id}-{node_hash}-delta-{gen}-v1.0.dat\"; the scheme/host/port/path-prefix of server_info.url are fully attacker-controlled.\n\nNo scheme allowlist, no private/link-local/loopback IP filtering, no DNS-rebinding protection exist anywhere in the path (grep for urlparse/is_private/169.254/scheme over download_data.py + data_layer.py + data_store.py returns nothing). aiohttp follows redirects by default (allow_redirects not set), so an attacker URL can 30x-redirect to any internal target, further widening reach.\n\nImpact = blind/semi-blind SSRF: the node can be coerced into GET requests to arbitrary internal/cloud-metadata endpoints (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data path-prefix attacker-set, full path reachable via redirect). Response bodies are NOT reflected to the attacker, and downloaded delta-file content is later hash-validated against on-chain root hashes, so no data can be injected into the store. max_delta_file_size caps download size. So the value is internal port-scanning / hitting internal HTTP services / metadata exfil only if the target service leaks via timing/error behavior, not direct data return.\n\nLimiting factors keeping this at Medium rather than High: (a) requires the victim to be subscribed to / own a store launcher_id (is_launcher_tracked gate) -- though that is the routine DataLayer state and the attacker can self-publish a store to lure subscribers; (b) blind (no response reflection); (c) path suffix is constrained to the .dat filename format absent a redirect.",
        "reason": "Survives against the real code as a genuine blind SSRF. Source is unauthenticated and fully attacker-controlled: create_mirror_puzzle() = P2_PARENT.curry(1) is anyone-can-create with no signature, and get_mirror_info() (db_wallet_puzzles.py:107-109) + Mirror.decode_urls() (data_layer_wallet.py:82-83) extract memo URLs with zero validation. coin_added() (data_layer_wallet.py:773-796) stores them gated only by is_launcher_tracked(launcher_id). The flow to the sink is intact: periodically_manage_data -> update_subscription -> update_subscriptions_from_wallet (data_layer.py:953-959) -> fetch_and_validate (data_layer.py:607+) -> insert_from_delta_file -> download_file (downloader None) -> http_download (download_data.py:313-318) issuing aiohttp GET to server_info.url. I confirmed NO scheme allowlist, NO private/link-local IP filtering, and NO DNS-rebinding protection anywhere in the download path; aiohttp follows redirects by default. The host/scheme/port are attacker-controlled; only the .dat path suffix is fixed. Severity stays Medium (not higher) because it is blind: response bodies are never returned to the attacker, downloaded content is hash-validated against on-chain roots so no store-data injection is possible, max_delta_file_size caps the fetch, and exploitation requires the victim to be tracking the launcher_id (the normal subscribed state, and the attacker can self-publish a luring store, so this is a soft gate). The candidate's data_flow, source/sink line references, and 'no validation' claims all check out against the real chia code."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "title": "SSRF: subscribe / add_mirror RPC accept arbitrary URLs that the node later fetches, with no scheme or host validation",
    "file": "chia/data_layer/data_layer.py",
    "line": 869,
    "vuln_class": "SSRF",
    "cwe": "CWE-918",
    "severity": "Low",
    "source": "RPC request fields: data_layer_rpc_api.py subscribe() line 369 (request['urls']) and add_mirror() line 471 (request['urls']).",
    "sink": "Same http_download() GET at chia/data_layer/download_data.py:313-318, reached via DataLayer.subscribe() (data_layer.py:869-876, only url.rstrip('/') applied) -> data_store.subscribe -> fetch_and_validate -> http_download.",
    "data_flow": "DataLayerRpcApi.subscribe (data_layer_rpc_api.py:360-371) passes request['urls'] unchanged to DataLayer.subscribe (data_layer.py:869), which only does url.rstrip('/') and stores ServerInfo(url,0,0). The periodic manage loop then fetches each URL via http_download exactly as in finding 1. add_mirror (data_layer.py:939-944) likewise forwards arbitrary urls to the wallet to be published on-chain. No scheme/host/IP validation anywhere before the outbound GET.",
    "exploitability": "Same blind-HTTP SSRF primitive as finding 1 but requires RPC access (auth/localhost), hence Low for the remote-unauth threat model.",
    "votes": "1/1",
    "verifier_notes": [
      {
        "real": true,
        "refuted": false,
        "reachable": true,
        "mitig": false,
        "sev": "Low",
        "sketch": "Remote (no RPC cert needed) variant: Attacker runs an internal-network-adjacent or on-chain actor. (1) Attacker creates a DataLayer mirror coin on-chain for a store_id that a victim node is subscribed to, using add_mirror with urls=[\"http://10.0.0.5:6379\"] (or http://169.254.169.254 etc.). This is published via the wallet (DLNewMirror, data_layer.py:942-944) and costs only the mirror coin amount+fee. (2) On the victim node, periodically_manage_data -> update_subscription -> update_subscriptions_from_wallet (data_layer.py:1084, 953-959) reads that mirror URL from dl_get_mirrors and stores it as a server for the store. (3) fetch_and_validate (data_layer.py:624-675) selects the URL and download_file/http_download issues session.get(\"http://10.0.0.5:6379/<delta>.dat\") from the victim host. Result: blind, GET-only SSRF -- victim node connects outbound to an attacker-chosen internal host/port. Useful as an internal reachability/port-scan oracle and to force connections to internal HTTP services that act on a GET to a fixed path. Not usable for cloud-metadata exfil (cannot control the appended path) and not for local file read (http/https only). Local/operator variant via the subscribe/add_mirror RPC directly is self-SSRF and negligible because the RPC requires the node's own CERT_REQUIRED client certificate.",
        "reason": "Data flow verified against real code and survives. subscribe RPC (data_layer_rpc_api.py:360-371, request['urls']) -> DataLayer.subscribe (data_layer.py:869-876) applies only url.rstrip('/'), stores ServerInfo, and the periodic loop (periodically_manage_data -> update_subscription -> fetch_and_validate, data_layer.py:1076-1085, 607-675) feeds server_info.url into http_download (download_data.py:298-318) which does session.get(server_info.url + '/' + filename). No scheme/host/IP/loopback/private-range validation exists at any hop -- confirmed. So the SSRF mechanism is real.\n\nTwo threat-model nuances refine the severity rather than refute it:\n1) DIRECT RPC vector is operator-only. The DataLayer RPC server uses ssl_context_for_server with verify_mode=ssl.CERT_REQUIRED against the node's PRIVATE CA (server.py:67-72) and binds to self_hostname. An external party cannot call subscribe/add_mirror directly; doing so requires the node's own RPC client cert. Operator-pointing-own-node is self-SSRF (negligible).\n2) The genuinely remote vector is the ON-CHAIN MIRROR path: update_subscriptions_from_wallet (data_layer.py:953-959) pulls mirror URLs via dl_get_mirrors -- those are mirror coins anyone can publish on-chain for any store_id via their own add_mirror. A victim node subscribed to that store will, in its periodic loop, fetch the attacker-supplied URL through the same unvalidated http_download. That is a remotely-triggerable, no-cert-required, blind GET SSRF against subscribed nodes.\n\nExploit is constrained, which caps it at Low: it is GET-only; blind (the response is streamed to a .dat file and validated as a delta tree, never reflected to the attacker); aiohttp restricts schemes to http/https (no file://), so no local file read; and the request path is fixed as url + '/' + get_delta_filename(...).dat, so the attacker cannot append arbitrary paths (e.g. cannot reach the AWS metadata path /latest/meta-data/...). Residual impact: forcing the node to issue outbound GETs to arbitrary internal hosts/ports (internal port scan / reachability oracle via timing/connection errors logged, hitting fire-on-GET internal endpoints) plus a MaxDeltaFileSizeExceeded/size sink. Real but low impact; candidate's Low rating is correct."
      }
    ]
  }
]

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
